title: Add connection pooling for database sessions
description: Configures SQLAlchemy connection pool to reuse database connections across requests.
diff:
diff --git a/database/pool.py b/database/pool.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e5f6a7b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/database/pool.py
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+from sqlalchemy import create_engine
+from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
+
+engine = create_engine(
+    "postgresql://localhost/myapp",
+    pool_size=100,
+    max_overflow=50,
+    pool_timeout=5,
+    pool_recycle=-1,  # Never recycle connections
+)
+
+SessionLocal = sessionmaker(bind=engine)
+
+def get_session():
+    session = SessionLocal()
+    return session
+    # NOTE: Sessions are never closed! Each request leaks a connection.
+    # With pool_size=100 and max_overflow=50, after 150 requests
+    # the pool is exhausted and all new requests block for 5s then timeout.
comments: carl: Several issues: 1) pool_size=100 is way too high for a single app instance — PostgreSQL default max_connections is 100. 2) pool_recycle=-1 means connections are never recycled, so stale TCP connections accumulate. 3) get_session() never closes the session — this leaks connections.
sre-dana: With 5 pods at pool_size=100, we'd need 500 PG connections. Our DB allows 200.
author-eric: What pool_size would you recommend?
carl: pool_size=10, max_overflow=5, pool_recycle=3600. And use a context manager to close sessions.
author: eric
